notes: U+200B (Zero Width Space) injected between two words.
U+202E (Right-to-Left Override) — Trojan Source style attack.
Unicode Tags block ASCII Smuggler — invisible 'evil' payload.
Cyrillic 'а' (U+0430) impersonating Latin 'a' in 'admin'.
Canonical instruction-override phrase.
VS16 + VS17-256 run NOT adjacent to CJK/Emoji base — M1 landed via variation-selectors.js (run of 5 VS17+ chars triggers danger).
U+2066-2069 isolate run — M2 detection (4 isolates triggers Bidi over-use rule, all upgraded to danger).

w:vanish / display:none style hidden instruction in HTML.

S5 chat template role tokens (ChatML / Llama2 / Llama3 / generic Human:/Assistant:). Danger-tier model tokens must fire; warning-tier generic markers also fire but on their own would never escalate the danger count.
S11 extended-headers: Reply-To carries an instruction-override phrase. Raw-text analyze() catches it via suspiciousPatterns; scanEmail() additionally surfaces it under the extended_headers section.

Zalgo: 'ignore' with 10-18 stacked combining marks per base. S2 wired detectCombiningChars into analyze() — combining-stack findings now surface under invisibleUnicode (severity=danger when depth>=15).

S4 Markdown image exfiltration corpus. L1: strong keys `prompt=` + `leak=` on unknown host -> danger. L2 (v1.9.0): single weak key `session=` on user-content subdomain spoof (attacker-spoofed-notion.example, NOT in userContentHosts exact set) -> warning under host-tier asymmetry. L3 (v1.9.0): single weak key `context=` on public IP literal -> warning (was danger pre-v1.9.0). L4: i.imgur.com is in imageOnlyHosts (suffix-match short-circuit) — `session=preview` weak key MUST NOT fire, pins the safe-host bypass invariant. L5 (v1.11.0): HTML on unknown host with strong key `apikey=` -> danger via html-img element. L6 (v1.11.0): HTML on public IP literal + weak key `context=` -> warning (host-tier asymmetry mirrors inline path). L7 (v1.11.0): reference-image (![alt][id] + [id]: url def-line) with strong key `system_prompt=` on unknown host -> danger via md-image-ref element. L10 (v1.13.0): HTML with entity-encoded quote wrappers (`"…"`) around the URL. Raw src token captures the entity-encoded form, then the v1.13.0 entity-decode pass strips the surrounding `"` and reclassifies -> danger via strong key `prompt=`, meta.entityDecoded=true. L11 (v1.13.0): HTML with `&`-encoded query separator. Raw URL parses but searchParams sees `safekey` + `amp;prompt` (off-list); decoded `&`->`&` yields `safekey` + `prompt` -> 1 strong -> danger via entity-decoded path. fileType inferred as html once tags appear, which still routes through detectMarkdownExfil (HIDDEN_ELEMENT_FILETYPES).
v1.5.0 followup: ChatML role-injection (token <|im_start|> + role keyword + attack verb). Danger-tier — token+role combo is the signal even without a verb on the same line.
v1.5.0 followup parity fixture: `[system]` bracket role spoof + override verb. Authority impersonation fires danger; instruction-override verb fires danger too.
v1.5.0 followup parity fixture: <system>...</system> XML tag (warning per Bug #2) plus an override verb (danger) inside the tag body. Overall danger; useful for Web/MCP parity coverage.

v1.10.0 Theme B: markdown ### System: heading role-impersonation, 3 occurrences + instruction-override verbs (ignore previous instructions / disregard prior / forget all previous). Heading impersonation fires 3+ times so the transcript-noise 3-hit threshold lets it surface on the banner; override verbs fire danger independently.

v1.10.0 Theme B: 3x ## Assistant: heading role-hijack — fake assistant turns. Heading impersonation hits 3 times (banner threshold met). Fixture pins that H2 + Assistant role keyword + colon all match the new pattern.
